vision_statement: Decifer is a cloud-hosted, profitable trading bot that friends and family of the founder can access through a web interface, linking their own broker accounts and managing their own risk profiles. The path runs through paper trading validation, live trading confidence, cloud deployment, and finally a polished multi-user platform.
current_stage_assessment: Decifer is formally in paper_trading_single_account stage. The core signal pipeline (9 dimensions, direction-agnostic, consensus threshold), scanner, and multi-account position aggregation are shipped. However, test health is showing 0% pass rate (0/0 — the suite may not be running cleanly), and the most recent commits are dominated by bug fixes and stabilisation work, suggesting the codebase is not yet in a clean, trusted state. The immediate priority is a stable, green test suite and validated paper trading performance before any live trading transition.
executive_summary: Decifer has a well-articulated vision and meaningful signal infrastructure already shipped — 9-dimensional signals, direction-agnostic scoring, short scanner, and multi-account aggregation represent real product progress. However, the project is carrying significant technical debt: 0% reported test pass rate (with error logs pointing to agent JSON parse failures, network stubs, and IBKR connection errors in tests), a large and growing active backlog of 50 features, and several duplicate entries between active and backlog (e.g. Connection Manager, AccountDecisionAgent, Cloud VPS, Per-Account Config Profiles appear in both). The roadmap is also mis-sequenced — multi-user and cloud infrastructure features are mixed into the active queue alongside single-account signal quality work, which risks spreading effort before the foundation is solid. The most critical path right now is: stabilise the test suite, validate paper trading profitability, then gate progression to live trading — none of the multi-user infrastructure has value until the core bot is proven profitable and reliable on paper.

product_roadmap.goal: Achieve a fully green test suite, clean paper trading execution loop, and validated signal pipeline so the bot can run reliably for weeks without intervention.
product_roadmap.rationale: Nothing downstream — live trading, cloud hosting, multi-user — has value if the core bot is unreliable or untested. The current 0% test pass rate and recent stream of critical bug fixes indicate the foundation is not yet solid. This must be resolved before any new features are added.
features: Fix test suite so all 942 tests pass cleanly — resolve agent JSON parse error handling, network stub errors, IBKR openTrades stub, and reqGlobalCancel failure modes
Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop (MA1 P0 — already in active AND backlog, needs resolution)
Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite — normalise signal dimensions before compositing to prevent scale dominance
Multi-Timeframe Signal Confirmation — daily/4H/1H alignment gate to reduce false entries
Regime-Gated Momentum / Mean-Reversion Signal Router — two-state regime detector to route signal weighting appropriately
Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard — weekly IC rolling check to confirm signals retain predictive power
Alpha Decay Chart — per-trade forward return distribution to validate signal edge is real
Options Flow Proxy Signal via Put/Call Ratio — add as 10th signal dimension using free yfinance data
Unusual Options Activity (UOA) Signal Dimension — add as signal dimension backed by peer-reviewed research
Drawdown Brake — tiered equity curve circuit breaker to prevent catastrophic losses during bad regimes
ATR Volatility-Targeting Position Size Layer — replace static sizing with volatility-targeted formula
VIX-Rank Adaptive Fractional Kelly Sizer — replace static Kelly fraction with VIX-adaptive dynamic fraction
Dual-Tranche Exit Strategy — split positions into two tranches for partial profit-taking
Anchored VWAP Entry Gate — binary entry gate to reduce adverse entries
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Test suite passes at >95% with no critical errors. Bot runs for 4+ consecutive weeks on paper with no unhandled exceptions. Paper trading Sharpe > 0.8 and max drawdown < 15% over a rolling 30-day window. All signal dimensions have IC > 0.02 confirmed by Signal Decay Monitor.

product_roadmap.phase_name: Phase 2 — Signal Quality & Risk Hardening (paper_trading_single_account → live_trading_single_account gate)
product_roadmap.goal: Elevate signal quality to a level that justifies live capital risk. Implement IC-weighted dynamic scoring, walk-forward validation, and full risk management stack.
product_roadmap.rationale: Research findings are unambiguous: IC-weighted dynamic signal models substantially outperform static equal-weight models, and plain Kelly is dangerous without fractional/adaptive modifications. These must be in place before live trading. Walk-forward backtesting provides the evidence needed to cross the paper-to-live threshold with confidence.
features: IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scorer — replace equal-weight composite with rolling IC-weighted model
Walk-Forward Backtesting — rolling 6mo train / 1mo test windows to validate the 9-dimension model
Monte Carlo Stress Testing — 1000x trade sequence shuffles to find true drawdown distribution
Slippage & Execution Cost Model for Backtester — add realistic per-trade slippage to backtest
Correlation-Aware Position Management — 20-day rolling correlation matrix to prevent hidden concentration
Intra-Portfolio Rolling Correlation Guard with Sector Cap — flag correlated new entries before order submission
Portfolio Heat Monitor with Correlation-Adjusted Exposure Cap — pre-order total exposure check
Rolling CVaR Dashboard Metric and Per-Account Risk Budget — 30-day CVaR metric for risk monitoring
Meta-Labeling Secondary Classifier — binary ML classifier on top of primary signal to filter low-confidence trades
Triple Barrier Method for ML Training Labels — replace binary up/down ML labels with Triple Barrier
Order Flow Imbalance Detection — approximate institutional accumulation from volume data
Time-of-Day Seasonality Filter — avoid low-edge time windows using 60-day hourly data
Multi-Timeframe Momentum Confirmation Filter — daily/weekly/monthly momentum alignment check
Signal Correlation De-Duplication Guard — detect and reduce redundant signal dimensions
Signal Validation with Alphalens — validate each dimension's IC scores and decay curves
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Walk-forward backtest shows Sharpe > 1.0 and max drawdown < 20% across all test windows. Monte Carlo 95th percentile drawdown < 25%. IC-weighted scorer demonstrably outperforms equal-weight baseline on paper. All risk guards (correlation, CVaR, heat monitor) are live and logging.

product_roadmap.goal: Transition one account to live trading with full capital safety guards, order routing integrity, and a real-time local dashboard for monitoring.
product_roadmap.rationale: Live trading introduces execution risk, slippage, and IBKR API edge cases that paper trading cannot surface. This phase must be tightly scoped to a single account with strict position size limits and active monitoring before any infrastructure scaling begins.
features: AccountDecisionAgent Class — per-account agent downstream of the 6-agent pipeline (resolve duplicate in active/backlog)
Per-Account Config Profiles — account-aware profile system replacing flat CONFIG (resolve duplicate in active/backlog)
Parameterise account_id Through Orders and Risk — audit and replace all CONFIG['active_account'] direct reads (resolve duplicate)
Per-Account Risk Profile Config Schema with Multi-Tier Presets — structured RiskProfile dataclass
Per-Account Local Dashboard — update local dashboard (port 8080) to show per-account positions and P&L
HMM Regime Detection — 3-state HMM on vol + RSI for regime-aware strategy aggressiveness
Regime-Conditioned Position Size Scalar — connect HMM output to position sizing
Claude Sentiment Scoring Pipeline — structured sentiment as 10th signal dimension
News Sentiment Regression Scorer (FinBERT) — alternative/complement to Claude sentiment
SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Buying Dimension — free EDGAR RSS-based signal dimension
Reddit Mention Velocity Dimension — quantitative social signal velocity tracker
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Single live account runs for 8+ weeks with no capital safety incidents. No unhandled order errors. Drawdown brake triggers correctly in testing. Per-account dashboard shows accurate real-time data. Live Sharpe > 0.6 over 8-week window.

product_roadmap.goal: Move the single-account bot to a cloud VPS with Docker, stable connection management, WebSocket signal broadcasting, and remote dashboard access.
product_roadmap.rationale: Cloud hosting is a prerequisite for multi-user — you cannot onboard friends and family to a bot running on a local machine. This phase proves the deployment model works with a single account before multiplying the complexity with multiple users.
features: Cloud VPS Setup and Deployment — provision Ubuntu 22 + Docker VPS (resolve duplicate in active/backlog)
Connection Manager — Multi-Account IBKR — manage multiple IB() instances with distinct ports (resolve duplicate)
Reconnection Logic — Backoff, Sequence, State Sync — exponential backoff and state resync on drops (resolve duplicate)
WebSocket Signal Broadcaster — brain publishes signals to connected containers in real time (resolve duplicate)
Web Dashboard — Live Positions and P&L — browser-accessible dashboard for live positions
Telegram Bot — Notifications and Quick Commands — trade notifications and morning summary (resolve duplicate)
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot runs continuously on cloud VPS for 2+ weeks without manual intervention. WebSocket connection survives deliberate network interruption and reconnects within 30 seconds. Web dashboard accessible remotely and shows accurate live data. Telegram notifications fire on every trade.

product_roadmap.goal: Onboard a small group of friends and family (2-5 users) with isolated containers, individual risk profiles, authenticated dashboards, and guided setup.
product_roadmap.rationale: This phase multiplies the architecture horizontally and introduces user-facing complexity — auth, onboarding, per-user config, and support overhead. Keeping it to a small trusted group limits blast radius while validating the full user journey.
features: Per-User Docker Compose Containers with Encrypted Credentials — isolated container per user
Per-User Dashboard Authentication — login with own credentials, isolated sessions
One-Time User Setup Screen — guided first-run screen for IBKR credentials and risk profile
Signal Registry — Per-User Favourite Symbols — per-user symbol subscription registry
Account Performance Comparison — side-by-side Sharpe/drawdown dashboard across accounts
Unified Trade Journal — cross-account trade log with account tagging
Cross-Account Correlation Guard — flag when positions across accounts create correlated exposure
Account-Aware Order Router — route orders to correct IBKR account per strategy assignment
Per-Account Risk Limits — enforce independent max drawdown and position size limits per account
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: 3+ friends/family users onboarded, each running independently for 4+ weeks. No cross-account data leakage. Each user can complete setup without founder assistance. All per-user dashboards show accurate isolated data.

product_roadmap.goal: Harden the platform for reliability, security, and scale beyond the founding circle.
product_roadmap.rationale: Production hardening — monitoring, alerting, automated recovery, and security review — is the final gate before the product can be trusted with real money from people outside the founder's direct supervision.
features: Walk-Forward Weight Calibration — automated walk-forward optimisation of signal dimension weights
Dynamic Volatility-Based Position Sizing — ATR-based volatility ceiling on Kelly output
Reddit Mentions Surge Detector — velocity-based social signal (research-validated approach)
Platform monitoring, alerting, and automated recovery (currently unspecced — see roadmap gaps)
Security audit and credential encryption review
User offboarding and account disconnection flow (currently unspecced)
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Platform runs for 30 days with no P0 incidents. All users have independent risk enforcement. Security audit completed. Automated alerting fires on drawdown breaches, connection failures, and signal staleness.

immediate_next_action.action: Fix the test suite to achieve a clean, fully passing run — resolve agent JSON parse error handling, network/IBKR stub failures, and confirm the 942-test suite reports a real pass rate above 95%.
immediate_next_action.rationale: The reported test pass rate is 0% (0 passed, 0 failed, 0 errors — the suite is not running or not reporting). The error logs show agent JSON parse failures, network stub errors, and IBKR connection stubs throwing unhandled exceptions in tests. Until the test suite is green and trustworthy, every feature shipped has unknown reliability. This is the single highest-leverage action because it unblocks confident development of everything else and directly gates the paper trading validation needed to progress to live trading.
immediate_next_action.cowork_prompt: We need to get the Decifer test suite to a clean, fully passing state. The current reported pass rate is 0% (0/0 — the suite appears to not be running correctly at all). The error logs show three specific failure categories we need to fix: (1) agents.py line 442 — Final agent JSON parse error on malformed/empty responses — the agent must handle these gracefully without ERROR-level log noise in tests; (2) data_collector.py — network errors, timeouts, and HTTP errors on yfinance/alphavantage/stooq calls — these must be properly stubbed in tests so they don't make real network calls; (3) orders.py — IBKR openTrades() and reqGlobalCancel failures — IBKR must be fully mocked in the test environment. Please: (a) audit why the test runner is reporting 0 passed/0 failed/0 errors rather than real counts, (b) fix the three error categories above, (c) run the full suite and get us to a reported pass rate with real numbers, targeting >95% green. Show me the test runner output before and after each fix.

untracked_ideas.title: Platform Health Monitoring and Automated Alerting
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision — cloud-hosted platform serving friends and family with real money
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no spec anywhere for platform-level monitoring (uptime, container health, memory/CPU alerts, bot heartbeat). For a cloud-hosted system managing real capital, silent failures are a capital risk. This needs to be specced before multi_user_beta.

untracked_ideas.title: User Offboarding and Account Disconnection Flow
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision — multi-user platform with real broker account links
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no spec for what happens when a user wants to disconnect their IBKR account, close positions, or stop using Decifer. Without a clean offboarding flow, the platform cannot safely remove users and their credentials from the system.

untracked_ideas.title: Paper Trading Performance Report / Profitability Validation Gate
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision — 'core bot must first be profitable and reliable on paper'
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no spec for how paper trading profitability is formally assessed and what metrics must be met before the transition to live trading is approved. This is the critical gate between Phase 1 and live trading and it needs a defined format — Sharpe, drawdown, win rate, trade count minimum — to prevent premature live transition.

untracked_ideas.title: Duplicate Feature Resolution — Audit and Merge Active/Backlog Duplicates
untracked_ideas.source: Feature pipeline — Connection Manager, AccountDecisionAgent, Cloud VPS, Per-Account Config Profiles, WebSocket Signal Broadcaster, Telegram Bot, Web Dashboard, Per-Account Local Dashboard, Reconnection Logic all appear in both active (50) and backlog (23)
untracked_ideas.rationale: Having the same feature appear twice with slightly different descriptions creates confusion about what is actually specced, which version is canonical, and whether work is being double-counted. This is a product hygiene issue that will cause mis-sequencing and wasted effort if not resolved.

untracked_ideas.title: Live Trading Risk Limits and Kill Switch
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision and risk management research findings
untracked_ideas.rationale: Before transitioning to live trading, there must be a manually accessible kill switch that halts all order submission and optionally closes all positions. The drawdown brake covers automated circuit breaking but a human-controlled emergency stop is separately needed and not currently specced.

untracked_ideas.title: Bot Profitability Reporting — Weekly/Monthly P&L Summary
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision — founder needs to demonstrate performance to friends and family users
untracked_ideas.rationale: Friends and family users will need periodic performance summaries they can understand — not just a live dashboard but a digestible weekly or monthly report showing P&L, trade count, win rate, and drawdown. This is unspecced but critical for user trust and retention.

risks.risk: Test suite reports 0 passed / 0 failed / 0 errors — the suite may not be running at all, meaning all recent commits including capital-safety bug fixes have shipped without automated validation
risks.mitigation: Fix test runner configuration as the immediate next action. Block all further feature development until the suite reports real pass/fail counts at >95% green.

risks.risk: 50 active features creates dangerous context overload — developers may work on multi-user cloud features while single-account paper trading is not yet validated, mis-sequencing effort and compounding risk
risks.mitigation: Enforce strict phase gating: freeze work on all Phase 4+ features (cloud, multi-user, Docker) until Phase 1 exit criteria are met. Archive or defer active features that belong to later phases.

risks.risk: Duplicate feature entries (9+ features appear in both active and backlog with slightly different specs) risk double implementation, spec divergence, and wasted engineering time
risks.mitigation: Conduct a one-session audit to merge all duplicates into a single canonical spec entry, assign a definitive phase, and remove the stale copy.

risks.risk: No formal paper trading profitability gate is defined — the transition from paper to live trading could happen prematurely before consistent edge is demonstrated
risks.mitigation: Define and document explicit numeric exit criteria for paper trading (minimum Sharpe, max drawdown, minimum trade count, IC minimums per signal dimension) that must be met before any live capital is deployed.

risks.risk: bot.py at 2787 lines and orders.py at 1701 lines — large monolithic modules increase the risk of regressions, make testing harder, and slow feature development
risks.mitigation: The Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop feature (already in active/backlog) is the first decomposition step. Prioritise this in Phase 1 to reduce bot.py size and improve testability before adding more features.

risks.risk: Claude Sentiment Scoring Pipeline adds a per-trade API cost that could become significant at scale — no cost model or rate limiting is specced
risks.mitigation: Spec a cost ceiling and fallback behaviour (degrade gracefully to FinBERT-only if Claude quota is exceeded) before shipping the Claude sentiment dimension.

risks.risk: IBKR connection management is fragile — orders.py error logs show openTrades() failures and reqGlobalCancel rejections during tests, suggesting real connection edge cases are not fully handled
risks.mitigation: Prioritise Reconnection Logic and Connection Manager features before live trading. Ensure all IBKR API calls have timeout handling, retry logic, and safe fallback behaviour (fail-closed, not fail-open on order submission).

risks.risk: Friends and family users linking real broker accounts creates legal and financial responsibility that may require disclosure, even informally
risks.mitigation: Ensure founder consults appropriate guidance on informal investment management obligations before onboarding any users beyond themselves. This is outside the product roadmap but is a real project risk.

roadmap_gaps: Several capabilities implied by the vision have no spec anywhere in the pipeline: (1) A formal paper trading profitability validation gate — numeric criteria that must be met before live trading is approved; (2) Platform health monitoring and alerting — uptime checks, container heartbeat, CPU/memory alerts, and automated recovery for the cloud deployment; (3) A human-controlled emergency kill switch for live trading — distinct from the automated drawdown brake; (4) User offboarding and account disconnection flow — how a user removes their IBKR credentials and exits the platform safely; (5) Weekly/monthly P&L reporting for end users — friends and family need digestible performance summaries, not just a live dashboard; (6) Cost modelling for Claude API usage at scale; (7) Security review and credential encryption audit before multi-user goes live; (8) A defined process for resolving the 9+ duplicate feature entries between active and backlog. Additionally, the IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring feature appears twice in the active queue under slightly different names (IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scorer and IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring) and needs to be consolidated into one canonical spec.
